hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: Telling People What I Do -- My Role, My Team
lesson.menu_desc: A virada do curso: sair do I e falar dos OUTROS. Sete palavras de função (cover, manage, handle, visit, report, team, office), o present simple na 3ª pessoa -- she works / Does she work? -- e os possessivos (my, her, his), até descrever o cargo, o time e a gestora sem travar. -- 35 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- Telling People What I Do
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 4 -- Telling People What I Do
lesson.grammar_point: present simple -- 3rd person singular (she works / Does she work?) + possessive adjectives (my / your / her / his)

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. David here. You ask me about Sofia — so, here it is. Sofia manages our team. She covers the south region, and she handles the big accounts with you. She starts early, she reads my report on Monday, and she visits the brokers on Tuesday. She doesn't stay in the office all week — never. One more thing, and this is important: she doesn't want a perfect report. She wants a clear one. Her door is always open. Ask her anything. See you Monday.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. Welcome to my team — our team now. Let me tell you who does what. David is our analyst: he handles the numbers, and his report comes to me every Monday. My job is the team and the south region. Your job is the client — the big ones. You cover large risks, and that is twenty years of your life, so I am not worried. I visit the brokers every month, and you come with me on Tuesday. My office is on the third floor. Come and ask. Welcome, Paula.

reading.rtitle: Who Does What: The Commercial Team, São Paulo
paras: Sofia Reyes manages the commercial team. She covers the south region. Her team works with brokers and big clients, every day. She visits the brokers every month — she doesn't stay in the office all week.

Paula Morales is the new commercial professional. She covers large risks: banks, factories, hospitals. David is the analyst. He handles the numbers, and he writes the report every Monday. Does he visit the brokers? No, he doesn't. That is not his job.
This is Sofia, in her own words: "My team is small, and it works. I manage six people. I visit our brokers every month. Paula visits them with me now."

"Does she know insurance?" people ask about Paula. "Twenty years of it," Sofia answers. "She knows the client, she knows the risk, and she knows the market. My job is the team. Her job is the client. His job is the report. Everybody covers one thing, and together we cover everything."

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Everybody on the team does the same job, and Sofia checks the work.
The team page explains who does what — every person covers one part, and together they cover everything.
Paula is new at Zurich, so she does not know the insurance market yet.

items: Sofia manages the commercial team.
t
The first line says it: "Sofia Reyes manages the team." She also covers the south region.
Paula handles the numbers and writes the report.
f
No — that is David. "He handles the numbers, and he writes the report every Monday." Paula covers large risks.
Sofia visits the brokers every month.
t
She says it herself: "I visit our brokers every month." She doesn't stay in the office all week.
David visits the brokers too.
f
No, he doesn't. The text asks and answers it: "Does he visit the brokers? No, he doesn't. That is not his job."
Paula is new, so she does not know insurance.
f
She knows it. Sofia answers: "Twenty years of it. She knows the client, she knows the risk, and she knows the market."

bank.label: What I do / O que EU faço
items: I am responsible for large risks.
I cover big companies — banks, factories, hospitals.
I visit the brokers with Sofia.
My office is in São Paulo.

bank.label: What THEY do / O que os OUTROS fazem
items: My manager is Sofia. She manages the commercial team.
She visits the brokers every month. She doesn't stay in the office.
David is our analyst. He handles the numbers.
His report comes every Monday.
Does she visit the brokers? Yes, she does.

items.situation: A new colleague asks: "What do you do here, exactly?" Say what you are responsible for — and what you cover.
tips: I am responsible for large risks.
I cover big companies — banks, factories, hospitals.

items.situation: "Who is your manager?" Say her name — and say what she does. Two sentences.
Someone from People & Culture asks: "Does Sofia visit the brokers?" She does — every month. Answer with the short answer first, then the full sentence.
tips: She visits the brokers every month.

items.situation: "Does Paula write the report?" No — David does. Correct it: say no, then say who.
tips: David writes the report. He handles the numbers.

items.situation: Describe your team in two sentences: who manages it, and what it handles.
tips: My team handles big companies — large risks.

items.situation: You want to know what the other person does. Ask a question with "do" or "does".
tips: Does your team handle large risks?
